title: Walkthrough: Able Assess Falls Risk Tests

seo.title: How Able Assess Works: Four Falls Risk Tests in Five Minutes | Able Care
seo.description: See how Able Assess runs four validated falls risk tests in one five-minute screening any staff member can deliver. Grip strength, Timed Up & Go, gait speed, chair stand.

blocks.headline: Four falls risk tests. One five-minute screening.
blocks.subtitle: Run by any staff member. Validated against CDC STEADI and the 2022 World Guidelines for Falls Prevention. No clinical certification required.
blocks.backgroundImageAlt: Able Assess running on a tablet next to the GripAble sensor

blocks.heading: Four key falls risk assessments in five minutes
blocks.body:
One sensor, one app, one workflow. The same five-minute screening covers all four measurements the 2022 World Guidelines call for:

  • Single Maximum Grip Strength
  • Timed Up & Go Test
  • Four-Meter Gait Speed
  • 30-Second Chair Stand Test

Two minutes of footage shows what an actual screening looks like, from app open to participant report.

cards.title: The kit
cards.body: A GripAble sensor, a measuring tape, and the blue fanny pack that holds the sensor during the gait, balance, and chair-stand tests. Ships ready to use.

cards.title: The Able Assess app
cards.body: Walks any staff member through every test, step by step. Fifteen minutes of training, no clinical certification needed.

cards.title: The Able Assess portal
cards.body: See risk stratification across your population, and track changes for any individual over time. Export reports for care plans, family meetings, or audits.

steps.title: Open the app
steps.description: Pick the participant. The app walks you through the four tests in order. Every prompt is on screen. No script to memorize.

steps.title: Run the four tests
steps.description: For grip strength, the participant squeezes the sensor when the app says go. For gait speed, lower-limb strength, and balance, you measure the distance the app shows you, slip the sensor into the fanny pack, and follow the on-screen instructions to guide the participant.

steps.title: See results immediately
steps.description: The app stratifies each participant into low, intermediate, or high falls risk against age-banded norms. Print a participant-friendly paper report on the spot if needed.

steps.title: Track changes over time
steps.description: Every screening syncs to the Able Assess portal. Pull individual trends for care planning, or population reports for compliance, board, and operator reviews.

blocks.heading: Why these four tests
blocks.body: These four measurements together stratify risk more accurately than any single one. Grip strength is a peripheral marker of muscle mass, bone density, and biological resilience. Timed Up & Go captures dynamic balance. Four-meter gait speed measures functional locomotion. The 30-second chair stand isolates lower-limb strength. Captured together, they produce the multifactorial picture the 2022 World Guidelines for Falls Prevention call for, in five minutes instead of forty-five.

blocks.heading: See Able Assess in your workflow.
blocks.bodyText: A 20-minute demo with a clinician who has run it in the field.
